repo_notes.content: SCOPE: Generate EXACTLY two English-only pages: 'Skill Mapping' and 'Knowledge Base'. Zero Chinese text. Both pages serve as human-readable documentation AND as input for an automated extraction pipeline downstream.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: TAXONOMY FILE: The file .devin/taxonomy.yaml in this repo defines 8 tracks (T1-T8), each with 5 levels (L0-L4). Each level contains a 'skills' map of snake_case skill keys, each with an 'aliases' array. When mapping technologies to skills, use ONLY (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) pairs that exist in this file. Format: taxonomy_ref = 'T{1-8}-L{0-4}', skill_name = a key under that level's skills map. If a technology has no match, list it separately as an unmapped skill with a suggested track.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: STRUCTURED DATA PREFERENCE: Each page should contain ONE primary fenced YAML code block (```yaml ... ```) as its structured payload. All supplementary discussion should be OUTSIDE the code block as prose. If producing valid YAML is difficult, use a markdown table with columns [taxonomy_ref, skill_name, evidence_file, evidence_lines, summary] as an alternative. The downstream pipeline can parse either format.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: EVIDENCE RULES: Every skill claim must cite at least one source file path and approximate line range. Format: {file: 'path/to/file', lines: '42-87', summary: '...'}. Distinguish between 'declared dependency' (package.json/CMakeLists.txt only) and 'implemented/customized' (actual usage in source). Only implemented usage counts as proficiency evidence. If line numbers are uncertain, prefix with '~'. If path is inferred, append '[inferred]'.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: ATS KEYWORD SOURCING: The 'aliases' arrays in .devin/taxonomy.yaml are the PRIMARY source for exact ATS keywords. In the Skill Mapping output, separate keywords into: 'exact' (drawn from taxonomy aliases) and 'semantic' (additional terms observed in code — class names, variable names, API calls — that are NOT already in the aliases array). Do NOT duplicate taxonomy aliases into semantic.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

pages.title: Skill Mapping
pages.purpose: Identify ALL technologies, frameworks, libraries, algorithms, and design patterns in this codebase. Map each to the robotics skill taxonomy in .devin/taxonomy.yaml using (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) pairs. For each skill, provide file-level evidence and ATS keywords. Also document the tech stack (languages with approximate LOC%, frameworks, tools). List any technologies not covered by the taxonomy as unmapped skills.

page_notes.content: PREFERRED YAML STRUCTURE: {schema_version: 1, source_repo: '<repo>', skills: [{taxonomy_ref, skill_name, proficiency_evidence: [{file, lines, summary}], ats_keywords: {exact: [...], semantic: [...]}}], tech_stack: {languages: [{name, version, loc_pct}], frameworks: [...], tools: [...]}, unmapped_skills: [{name, observed_in, suggested_track}]}. After the YAML block, add prose sections: '## Architecture Context' and '## Implementation Patterns'.

pages.title: Knowledge Base
pages.purpose: Generate 5-8 resume bullet points derived from this codebase. Each bullet must start with a strong action verb, include one quantified metric (real or estimated from code), and reference at least one (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) pair from the Skill Mapping page. Also provide a project summary, architectural decisions, and interview talking points.

page_notes.content: PREFERRED YAML STRUCTURE: {schema_version: 1, project_title: '<string>', project_summary: '<2 sentences>', bullets: [{id: 'B01'..'B08', text: '<≤120 chars, action verb, one metric>', star: {situation, task, action, result}, skills_referenced: [{taxonomy_ref, skill_name}], ats_keywords: [...], interview_weight: <0.0-1.0>}]}. After the YAML block, add: '## Project Narrative' and '## Talking Points'.

BULLET QUALITY: (1) text must be the final resume line — ATS-scannable, action-verb-first, exactly one quantified metric. (2) Metrics must be derivable from code (throughput, latency, LOC, test coverage). Mark estimates with '[est.]'. (3) interview_weight 0.7-1.0 for strong metrics + deep complexity. (4) IDs sequential: B01-B08.
